question: San Diego FC vs. Minnesota United FC: O/U 2.5
description:
In the upcoming MLS game between San Diego FC and Minnesota United FC, scheduled for April 11 at 10:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if San Diego FC and Minnesota United FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: San Diego FC vs. Minnesota United FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 10:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota United FC holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their exhibition clash with expansion side San Diego FC, reflecting the Loons' established MLS pedigree against SDFC's unproven roster cohesion. Recent developments include Minnesota's mixed preseason form, including a 2-1 win over Chicago Fire but injuries to key midfielder Carlos Harvey (doubtful), while San Diego boasts new signings like Hirving "Chucky" Lozano and Marcus Ingvartsen for attacking flair yet faces integration challenges. Upcoming factors favor Minnesota's depth and home-field edge at Allianz Field, though experimental lineups in this non-competitive friendly introduce upset potential amid playoff prep momentum.

events.title: Newcastle United Jets FC vs. Adelaide United FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Australia A-League game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle Jets' dismal home form, with just one win in their last six A-League outings and heavy defensive concessions, anchors current trader consensus favoring Adelaide United. The visitors ride a three-match unbeaten streak, including a solid 2-1 road win, positioning them mid-table while Jets languish near the bottom. No major injuries reported from official updates, but Jets midfielder Apostolos Stamatelopoulos' fitness remains questionable per beat reports, potentially weakening their attack. Head-to-head history shows Adelaide edging recent encounters 3-2 over five games, with schedule rest advantage to the visitors amid playoff positioning pressures—traders weigh Jets' home crowd boost against momentum shift.

events.title: Colorado Rapids SC vs. Houston Dynamo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 9:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Rapids' robust home record at Dick's Sporting Goods Park drives trader sentiment, pricing a Rapids victory at around 55% implied probability amid their push for Western Conference supremacy. Rapids boast a 7-2-4 home mark this season, fueled by a four-game unbeaten run and Djordje Mihailovic's four goals in recent outings, per official team updates. Houston Dynamo struggle on the road (3-6-3), reeling from two straight losses and midfielder Hector Herrera's questionable status due to hamstring strain listed on the latest injury report. No major Rapids absences reported, but Dynamo's defensive vulnerabilities—conceding 1.8 goals per away game—heighten upset potential if they exploit counters, with playoff positioning amplifying stakes.

events.title: Auckland FC vs. Melbourne Victory FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Australia A-League game, scheduled for April 11 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Melbourne Victory's superior A-League experience and attacking firepower anchor trader consensus, pricing them as favorites against expansion side Auckland FC in this cross-Tasman clash. Auckland sits atop the ladder unbeaten after two wins, boosted by home-soil advantage at Go Media Stadium and clinical finishing from Logan Rogerson, but Victory's recent 3-2 thriller over Sydney FC highlights their resilience and stars like Bruno Fornaroli. No major injuries reported on official lists, though Auckland's defense faces test from Victory's high press. Momentum tilts toward the visitors' historical edge in big games, with odds reflecting crowd wisdom on their depth amid Auckland's untested squad. Schedule rest favors both post-midweek internationals.

events.title: FC Dallas vs. St. Louis City SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis City SC's dismal away form—winless in nine road MLS matches this season—anchors trader consensus favoring FC Dallas at home, with markets reflecting about a 55% implied probability for a Dallas win or draw. FC Dallas sits higher in the Western Conference standings (8th vs. St. Louis' 13th), bolstered by recent momentum including a 2-1 upset over LA Galaxy and full fitness for key midfielder Asier Illarramendi after minor knock recovery per official reports. St. Louis counters with striker João Klauss questionable (hamstring strain, per beat reporters), weakening their attack amid a three-match skid. Upcoming factors include Dallas' rest edge post-midweek bye and intense rivalry stakes in Toyota Stadium's thin air, though St. Louis' counter speed could spark upset potential.

events.title: Sporting Kansas City vs. San Jose Earthquakes - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Jose Earthquakes' league-worst record and defensive collapse fuel trader consensus heavily toward Sporting Kansas City in their October 19 home clash at Children's Mercy Park. The Quakes sit dead last in the Western Conference at 6-21-6, leaking 73 goals amid a five-match losing streak and dismal 1-12-3 road form. Sporting KC (10-14-9, ninth in West) counters with a respectable 6-4-6 home ledger, recent wins over Houston and Minnesota boosting momentum in their fading playoff chase. No major injuries per official reports, though SKC's Khiry Shelton is sidelined; head-to-head dominance (four straight SKC wins) underscores the mismatch, with markets pricing SKC's attack against SJ's frailty.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Jose Earthquakes' league-worst record and defensive collapse fuel trader consensus heavily toward Sporting Kansas City in their October 19 home clash at Children's Mercy Park. The Quakes sit dead last in the Western Conference at 6-21-6, leaking 73 goals amid a five-match losing streak and dismal 1-12-3 road form. Sporting KC (10-14-9, ninth in West) counters with a respectable 6-4-6 home ledger, recent wins over Houston and Minnesota boosting momentum in their fading playoff chase. No major injuries per official reports, though SKC's Khiry Shelton is sidelined; head-to-head dominance (four straight SKC wins) underscores the mismatch, with markets pricing SKC's attack against SJ's frailty.

question: Sporting Kansas City vs. San Jose Earthquakes: O/U 2.5
description:
In the upcoming MLS game between Sporting Kansas City and San Jose Earthquakes, scheduled for April 11 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Sporting Kansas City and San Jose Earthquakes combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Vancouver Whitecaps FC vs. New York City FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vancouver Whitecaps hold a strong home edge at BC Place, where they've won 70% of recent MLS matches, fueling trader optimism for an upset against visiting New York City FC, whose road form lags at 40% wins this season. Recent developments include Whitecaps striker Brian White returning from a minor hamstring tweak per official injury report, boosting their attack that scored 2.5 goals per game at home lately, while NYCFC midfielder Keaton Parks is questionable with a calf strain, potentially weakening midfield control. NYCFC's four-match unbeaten streak tempers the market, but Whitecaps' rest advantage after a bye week and playoff positioning needs could shift probabilities toward the hosts in this cross-conference clash.

events.title: Inter Miami CF vs. New York Red Bulls - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lionel Messi's availability anchors trader consensus for Inter Miami CF against New York Red Bulls, with markets pricing Miami's attack heavily on his recent form after returning from Copa América duties and ankle injury recovery. Miami sits atop the Eastern Conference with a five-match unbeaten streak, fueled by Luis Suárez's 16 goals, but Red Bulls boast the league's best defense (23 goals conceded) and sit second, riding a four-win run including a 2-1 victory over Miami in August. Key watch: Messi's minutes management amid playoff push, Red Bulls' home edge at Red Bull Arena, and Miami's road woes (4-6-4 away), tempering implied probabilities amid MLS unpredictability.

Charlotte regains forward Pep Biel from suspension, bolstering scoring punch. Both sides are eliminated from playoffs, muting intensity, though Charlotte's fresher legs from a lighter schedule could prove decisive amid Nashville's six-game skid. Head-to-head history shows frequent draws, underscoring upset potential.

events.title: Portland Timbers vs. Los Angeles FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 4:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: LAFC enters as trader consensus favorite against Portland Timbers on Decision Day at Providence Park, driven by their second-place Western Conference standing, three straight wins, and dominance in recent head-to-heads (winning the last three). Portland's five-game unbeaten streak and home-field edge provide counterbalance, but injuries loom large: leading scorer Felipe Mora is questionable with a hamstring issue, Diego Chara is out, potentially weakening midfield control. LAFC boasts full health with Denis Bouanga in top form. Playoff implications heighten stakes—Portland needs a result to secure a postseason spot—while LAFC eyes seeding, underscoring why crowds price Western Conference power over Timbers' momentum.

events.title: Austin FC vs. Los Angeles Galaxy - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: LA Galaxy's red-hot form atop the Western Conference, with four wins in their last five MLS matches including a 3-1 rout over Seattle, drives heavy trader favoritism into this Austin FC home clash. Austin, mired in a three-game winless skid and conceding 10 goals across those outings, faces mounting pressure from defensive lapses despite Sebastián Driussi's scoring threat. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Galaxy's Riqui Puig and Dejan Joveljić duo boasts 25 combined goals, contrasting Austin's porous backline. Home crowd edge offers Austin a shot at upset, yet historical head-to-head tilts Galaxy's way (3-1 aggregate last two meetings), tempering implied probabilities around 60% visitor win consensus.

Austin, mired in a three-game winless skid and conceding 10 goals across those outings, faces mounting pressure from defensive lapses despite Sebastián Driussi's scoring threat. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Galaxy's Riqui Puig and Dejan Joveljić duo boasts 25 combined goals, contrasting Austin's porous backline. Home crowd edge offers Austin a shot at upset, yet historical head-to-head tilts Galaxy's way (3-1 aggregate last two meetings), tempering implied probabilities around 60% visitor win consensus.

events.title: CF Montréal vs. Philadelphia Union - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Union's robust home form at Subaru Park, where they've won four of their last six MLS matches, underpins trader consensus favoring them against CF Montréal, who languish at the bottom of the Eastern Conference with just three road wins this season. Recent developments include Montréal's key midfielder Victor Wanyama sidelined by a hamstring injury per the official report, weakening their midfield control, while Union's Andre Blake returns from international duty to bolster the backline. Philly's recent 2-1 upset over Inter Miami highlights their momentum, contrasted by Montréal's three-match losing skid. Upcoming factors like Union's rest advantage after a midweek bye could tilt possession stats, though Montréal's counterattacking threat persists in high-stakes Eastern Conference clashes.

events.title: Toronto FC vs. FC Cincinnati - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for April 11 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Cincinnati's dominant road form and league-leading defense anchor trader consensus favoring them heavily against struggling Toronto FC, who sit near the bottom of the Eastern Conference with just three home wins all season. Cincinnati enters unbeaten in their last five MLS matches, bolstered by full fitness for star midfielder Luciano Acosta after official injury reports cleared him, while Toronto grapples with key absences including forward Federico Bernardeschi (doubtful, hamstring). Recent head-to-heads tilt Cincinnati's way (2-0-1), and their rest advantage post-midweek could exploit TFC's fatigue from a grueling schedule. Momentum and roster depth point to low-scoring affair, aligning with under bets in related markets, though Toronto's desperation for points adds upset risk.

question: Valorant: Fnatic vs ULF Esports (BO3) - VCT EMEA Group Omega
description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Fnatic and ULF Esports in the VCT EMEA Group Omega, initially scheduled for April 1 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Fnatic" if Fnatic win the match against ULF Esports.

This market will resolve to "ULF Esports" if ULF Esports win the match against Fnatic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fnatic enters as heavy favorites against ULF Esports in this VCT EMEA Group Omega BO3 matchup, driven by their dominant recent form including a 2-0 sweep over Team Heretics and top-four seeding from Stage 1. ULF, a promotion hopeful from Challengers, struggles with inconsistency, dropping a 0-2 decision to Karmine Corp amid roster adjustments and lower map win rates on key sites like Bind and Lotus. Trader consensus reflects Fnatic's 75% implied win probability, bolstered by home-region momentum and superior duelist Leo's 1.25 ACS, though ULF's aggressive sentinel play could force an upset if Fnatic falters on Haven. Watch for patch 9.0 impacts on agent metas.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment favors UNiTY esports in this BO3 Group A clash at the Journey tournament, with implied probabilities reflecting their higher HLTV ranking and stronger recent form, including a 2-0 sweep over weaker opponents last week. question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
